compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Audit OSS finding #2 (MEDIUM). The GitHub mirror has no CodeQL / SAST workflow. Security scanning today is gitleaks-driven secret detection on the Gerrit review path plus a gating pip-audit — good, but no static code analysis. CodeQL is free for public repos and runs fine on the read-only mirror (push/schedule triggers, writes results to the Security tab, does not touch the tree), adding an independent detection layer.

## What / files
Add `.github/workflows/codeql.yml`:
- `languages` matrix: exactly `python` and `javascript` (the CodeQL `javascript` key already covers TypeScript; do NOT use `javascript-typescript`, which is not the Actions matrix key).
- Triggers: `push` to `main` + a weekly `schedule` cron.
- Least-privilege `permissions`: `security-events: write`, `contents: read`, `actions: read`.
- `concurrency` with `cancel-in-progress: true`.
- Pin actions by MAJOR VERSION TAG to match repo convention (test.yml/gerrit-verify use `actions/checkout@v5`, `actions/setup-python@v6`); use `github/codeql-action/init@v3` / `analyze@v3`. No SHA-pinning (no other workflow here uses it).
- Alerts route to the default Security tab.

## Scope
In: `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` (new file only). Out: changing other workflows' pinning; alert routing beyond the Security tab.

Out of scope as an acceptance gate — first-run triage: CodeQL's first scan only runs on the GitHub mirror AFTER this change lands on main, so first-run results cannot exist while the ticket is worked in the worktree. First-run triage is a tracked post-landing operational follow-up (recorded separately once the mirror run completes); it is deliberately NOT an acceptance criterion of this ticket, because it is not evaluable in-session and cannot distinguish a clean run from an errored one at close time.

## Acceptance Criteria (all in-session verifiable)
- [ ] `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` exists and parses as valid YAML (python -c "import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/codeql.yml'))" exits 0), with a `languages` matrix of exactly `python` and `javascript`
- [ ] `permissions` is least-privilege (exactly security-events: write, contents: read, actions: read); a weekly `schedule:` trigger exists in addition to `push:` on main; `concurrency` sets `cancel-in-progress: true`
- [ ] Every `uses:` in the file is pinned by version tag (`@vN`) — grep-verifiable that no `@main`, `@master`, or 40-hex-SHA pin appears
- [ ] The workflow uses `github/codeql-action/init` + `/analyze` (v3) and sets `category`/language matrix correctly (structural check: the two steps are present under the matrixed job)
compiled_state.title: Add a CodeQL workflow on the GitHub mirror (python + javascript)
